title: kubectl reference
text:
kubectl reference
Expand all
Collapse all
kubectl annotate
Update the annotations on a resource
View full reference →
kubectl api-resources
Print the supported API resources on the server
View full reference →
kubectl api-versions
Print the supported API versions on the server, in the form of "group/version"
View full reference →
kubectl apply
Apply a configuration to a resource by file name or stdin
kubectl apply edit-last-applied
— Edit latest last-applied-configuration annotations of a resource/object
kubectl apply set-last-applied
— Set the last-applied-configuration annotation on a live object to match the contents of a file
kubectl apply view-last-applied
— View the latest last-applied-configuration annotations of a resource/object
View full reference →
kubectl attach
Attach to a running container
View full reference →
kubectl auth
Inspect authorization
kubectl auth can-i
— Check whether an action is allowed
kubectl auth reconcile
— Reconciles rules for RBAC role, role binding, cluster role, and cluster role binding objects
kubectl auth whoami
— Experimental: Check self subject attributes
View full reference →
kubectl autoscale
Auto-scale a deployment, replica set, stateful set, or replication controller
View full reference →
kubectl certificate
Modify certificate resources
kubectl certificate approve
— Approve a certificate signing request
kubectl certificate deny
— Deny a certificate signing request
View full reference →
kubectl cluster-info
Display cluster information
kubectl cluster-info dump
— Dump relevant information for debugging and diagnosis
View full reference →
kubectl completion
Output shell completion code for the specified shell (bash, zsh, fish, or powershell)
View full reference →
kubectl config
Modify kubeconfig files
kubectl config current-context
— Display the current-context
kubectl config delete-cluster
— Delete the specified cluster from the kubeconfig
kubectl config delete-context
— Delete the specified context from the kubeconfig
kubectl config delete-user
— Delete the specified user from the kubeconfig
kubectl config get-clusters
— Display clusters defined in the kubeconfig
kubectl config get-contexts
— Describe one or many contexts
kubectl config get-users
— Display users defined in the kubeconfig
kubectl config rename-context
— Rename a context from the kubeconfig file
kubectl config set
— Set an individual value in a kubeconfig file
kubectl config set-cluster
— Set a cluster entry in kubeconfig
kubectl config set-context
— Set a context entry in kubeconfig
kubectl config set-credentials
— Set a user entry in kubeconfig
kubectl config unset
— Unset an individual value in a kubeconfig file
kubectl config use-context
— Set the current-context in a kubeconfig file
kubectl config view
— Display merged kubeconfig settings or a specified kubeconfig file
View full reference →
kubectl cordon
Mark node as unschedulable
View full reference →
kubectl cp
Copy files and directories to and from containers
View full reference →
kubectl create
Create a resource from a file or from stdin
kubectl create clusterrole
— Create a cluster role
kubectl create clusterrolebinding
— Create a cluster role binding for a particular cluster role
kubectl create configmap
— Create a config map from a local file, directory or literal value
kubectl create cronjob
— Create a cron job with the specified name
kubectl create deployment
— Create a deployment with the specified name
kubectl create ingress
— Create an ingress with the specified name
kubectl create job
— Create a job with the specified name
kubectl create namespace
— Create a namespace with the specified name
kubectl create poddisruptionbudget
— Create a pod disruption budget with the specified name
kubectl create priorityclass
— Create a priority class with the specified name
kubectl create quota
— Create a quota with the specified name
kubectl create role
— Create a role with single rule
kubectl create rolebinding
— Create a role binding for a particular role or cluster role
kubectl create secret
— Create a secret using a specified subcommand
kubectl create secret docker-registry
— Create a secret for use with a Docker registry
kubectl create secret generic
— Create a secret from a local file, directory, or literal value
kubectl create secret tls
— Create a TLS secret
kubectl create service
— Create a service using a specified subcommand
kubectl create service clusterip
— Create a ClusterIP service
kubectl create service externalname
— Create an ExternalName service
kubectl create service loadbalancer
— Create a LoadBalancer service
kubectl create service nodeport
— Create a NodePort service
kubectl create serviceaccount
— Create a service account with the specified name
kubectl create token
— Request a service account token
View full reference →
kubectl debug
Create debugging sessions for troubleshooting workloads and nodes
View full reference →
kubectl delete
Delete resources by file names, stdin, resources and names, or by resources and label selector
View full reference →
kubectl describe
Show details of a specific resource or group of resources
View full reference →
kubectl diff
Diff the live version against a would-be applied version
View full reference →
kubectl drain
Drain node in preparation for maintenance
View full reference →
kubectl edit
Edit a resource on the server
View full reference →
kubectl events
List events
View full reference →
kubectl exec
Execute a command in a container
View full reference →
kubectl explain
Get documentation for a resource
View full reference →
kubectl expose
Take a replication controller, service, deployment or pod and expose it as a new Kubernetes service
View full reference →
kubectl get
Display one or many resources
View full reference →
kubectl kuberc
Manage kuberc configuration files
kubectl kuberc set
— Set values in the kuberc configuration
kubectl kuberc view
— Display the current kuberc configuration
View full reference →
kubectl kustomize
Build a kustomization target from a directory or URL
View full reference →
kubectl label
Update the labels on a resource
View full reference →
kubectl logs
Print the logs for a container in a pod
View full reference →
kubectl options
Print the list of flags inherited by all commands
View full reference →
kubectl patch
Update fields of a resource
View full reference →
kubectl plugin
Provides utilities for interacting with plugins
kubectl plugin list
— List all visible plugin executables on a user's PATH
View full reference →
kubectl port-forward
Forward one or more local ports to a pod
View full reference →
kubectl proxy
Run a proxy to the Kubernetes API server
View full reference →
kubectl replace
Replace a resource by file name or stdin
View full reference →
kubectl rollout
Manage the rollout of a resource
kubectl rollout history
— View rollout history
kubectl rollout pause
— Mark the provided resource as paused
kubectl rollout restart
— Restart a resource
kubectl rollout resume
— Resume a paused resource
kubectl rollout status
— Show the status of the rollout
kubectl rollout undo
— Undo a previous rollout
View full reference →
kubectl run
Run a particular image on the cluster
View full reference →
kubectl scale
Set a new size for a deployment, replica set, or replication controller
View full reference →
kubectl set
Set specific features on objects
kubectl set env
— Update environment variables on a pod template
kubectl set image
— Update the image of a pod template
kubectl set resources
— Update resource requests/limits on objects with pod templates
kubectl set selector
— Set the selector on a resource
kubectl set serviceaccount
— Update the service account of a resource
kubectl set subject
— Update the user, group, or service account in a role binding or cluster role binding
View full reference →
kubectl taint
Update the taints on one or more nodes
View full reference →
kubectl top
Display resource (CPU/memory) usage
kubectl top node
— Display resource (CPU/memory) usage of nodes
kubectl top pod
— Display resource (CPU/memory) usage of pods
View full reference →
kubectl uncordon
Mark node as schedulable
View full reference →
kubectl version
Print the client and server version information
View full reference →
kubectl wait
Wait for a specific condition on one or many resources
View full reference →
Last modified March 22, 2026 at 3:35 PM PST:
Switch to Hugo page discovery and add description field to kubectl command front matter (83100f9a0d)
